title: The Negro's Place in American Life at the Present Day
venue: James Pott and Company, New York

license.note: Published 1903; public domain in the United States.

abstract: The Negro's Place in American Life at the Present Day (1903), by T. Thomas Fortune. From The Negro Problem.
excerpt: _ BY T. THOMAS FORTUNE Considering the two hundred and forty-five years of his slavery and the comparatively short time he has enjoyed the opportunities of freedom, his place in American life at the present day is creditable to him and promising for the future....
